name: German Rye Beer Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the yeast with the water. Let set for 5 minutes.
2. Add the warm beer to the mixture. Stir in the rye flour until smooth.
3. Add the caraway seeds and cover the bowl with a plate. Let rise for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Beat down the dough and gradually add the salt and unbleached flour. Mix well and knead for 6 - 8 minutes. If sticky, add a little flour on the board.
5. Grease the bowl and place the dough in it. Let rise until doubled in size.
6. Punch down the dough and shape into 2 rolls. Place the rolls in greased loaf pans (9 x 5 x 3 inches).
7. Make an egg wash using 1 egg yolk and a teaspoon of water. Brush on the rolls.
8. Slash deep slashes (1/4 inch) down each roll.
9. Let rise for 10 minutes, then preheat the oven to 350°F.
10. Bake for 15 minutes at 350°F, then increase the temperature to 400°F and bake for an additional 25 minutes.
11. Remove from the oven and let cool on wire racks for 10 minutes before serving.
